subject: [PATCH v2 0/2] mm/vmscan: reduce lru_lock contention via vmstat-derived scan-balance cost

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary:
The author acknowledged a need to modify the patch to increment counters for MGLRU as well, but noted that these counters are not used by MGLRU and is only for observability.

The author addressed a concern that the patch was missing updates to pgreclaim_pageout_* and pgrotate_* counters in MGLRU reclaim paths, which would misreport observability. The author confirmed this is indeed an issue and provided a fix by incrementing these counters in evict_folios().

2026-07-14.patch_summary: This patch reduces contention on the lru_lock by moving the cost-recording work to the reclaim side, where it is done lock-free using per-LRU vmstat counters and a dedicated cost_lock. The producer-side acquisitions of lru_lock are removed, reducing contention on memory-pressured workloads. The new scheme also improves the cost model across reclaim gaps by observing the whole between-reclaim delta and decaying anon and file proportionally.

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner
reviews.summary: Reviewer Johannes Weiner noted that using vm_event_state for tracking monotonic events is not feasible due to global events and lack of per-node tracking, but considers the current patch as a suitable solution.
reviews.raw_body:
Acked-by: Johannes Weiner <hannes@cmpxchg.org>

It's unfortunate that we mix state counts with monotonic events in
node_stat_item. We have vm_event_state for monotonics, but they aren't
tracked per-node (which we need here and for other places in vmscan),
and they can't easily be made so because there are "global" events in
there that don't easily map to a specific node.

So this seems like the best solution for now.

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner
reviews.summary: The reviewer pointed out that reclaim operations no longer write to file counters, suggesting using NR_VMSCAN_WRITE for anon counts instead.
reviews.raw_body:
Reclaim generally doesn't write files (anymore).

You can use NR_VMSCAN_WRITE for the anon counts.

2026-07-15.patch_summary: This patch reduces contention on the lru_lock by moving the cost-recording work to the reclaim side, where it is done lock-free using per-LRU vmstat counters and a dedicated cost_lock. The producer-side acquisitions of lru_lock are removed, reducing contention on memory-pressured workloads. The new scheme also improves the cost model across reclaim gaps by observing the whole between-reclaim delta and decaying anon and file proportionally.
